---
title: "Cloud Sandboxes for Inspect"
description: "A new package adding managed cloud sandbox providers to Inspect, so you can run evals at scale without provisioning your own infrastructure."
author: "Alexandra Abbas"
date: "2026-04-30"
image: cover.png
---

We're excited to announce [Inspect Sandboxes](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/), a new package that adds managed cloud sandbox providers ([Daytona](https://www.daytona.io/) and [Modal](https://modal.com)) to [Inspect AI](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/). Each sample runs in a fresh remote sandbox while the Inspect process runs locally; you don't need a Docker daemon on your machine or a cluster of your own.

Inspect already has [sandbox](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/sandboxing.html) providers for [Kubernetes](https://k8s-sandbox.aisi.org.uk/), [EC2](https://github.com/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_ec2_sandbox), and [Proxmox](https://github.com/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_proxmox_sandbox), but each requires you to provision and maintain your own infrastructure: a cluster, an AWS account, or a hypervisor host.

Over the last year, a wave of dedicated sandbox-provider companies has emerged to run AI-generated code at scale. Their workloads span code interpreters, coding agents, computer use, AI evaluations, and reinforcement learning training: anywhere a model needs a secure, ephemeral environment to execute code.

`inspect-sandboxes` brings that infrastructure to Inspect:

- [`daytona`](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/daytona.html) runs each sample on a managed Daytona sandbox. Supports single-container and multi-service compose files.
- [`modal`](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/modal.html) runs each sample on a serverless Modal sandbox. Single-container only.

```bash
pip install inspect-sandboxes
```

Pick one and point a task at it:

```python
from inspect_ai import Task, eval
from inspect_ai.agent import react
from inspect_ai.tool import bash, python

task = Task(
    dataset=[...],
    solver=react(tools=[bash(), python()]),
    sandbox="daytona",  # or modal
)

eval(task)
```

A `Dockerfile` or `compose.yaml` next to your task is auto-detected, or you can pass one explicitly: `sandbox=("daytona", "path/to/compose.yaml")`.

See the [docs](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/) for full configuration, including GPU selection, resource limits, network policies, and credentials.

::: {.callout-note title="Multi-service compose"}
Some agent benchmarks need more than a single container: a database, a victim service to attack, a verifier endpoint, or a tool runtime alongside the agent. A compose file with two or more services runs in Docker-in-Docker on Daytona, and each service is exposed as a separate `SandboxEnvironment`. Multi-service compose is not yet supported on Modal.
:::
